original_abstract: Controlling nanoparticle (NP) surface strain, i.e. compression (or stretch) of surface atoms, is an important approach to tune NP surface chemistry and to optimize NP catalysis for chemical reactions. Here we show that surface Pt strain in the core/shell FePt/Pt NPs with Pt in three atomic layers can be rationally tuned via core structural transition from cubic solid solution [denoted as face centered cubic (fcc)] structure to tetragonal intermetallic [denoted as face centered tetragonal (fct)] structure. The high activity observed from the fct-FePt/Pt NPs for oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) is due to the release of the overcompressed Pt strain by the fct-FePt as suggested by quantum mechanics-molecular mechanics (QM-MM) simulations. The Pt strain effect on ORR can be further optimized when Fe in FePt is partially replaced by Cu. As a result, the fct-FeCuPt/Pt NPs become the most efficient catalyst for ORR and are nearly 10 times more active in specific activity than the commercial Pt catalyst. This structure-induced surface strain control opens up a new path to tune and optimize NP catalysis for ORR and many other chemical reactions.
humanized_abstract: Surface strain or compression/stretch of surface atoms of nanoparticles (NPs) can be controlled to change the surface chemistry and enhance the catalytic activity of NPs for various chemical reactions. Here we report that the surface strain of Pt in the bimetallic core/shell structure FePt/Pt with three Pt layers can be rationally regulated by changing the structure of core (feet) from the cubic solid solution of face centered cubic (fcc) to the tetragonal structure of face centered tetragonal (fct). The significantly enhanced catalytic activity of the fct-FePt/Pt NPs for oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) is attributed to that fct-FePt relieves the over-compressed Pt strain in fcc-FePt according to the quantum mechanics-molecular mechanics (QM-MM) simulations. Furthermore, the Pt strain over catalytic activity of ORR can be enhanced by partially replacing Fe with Cu in FePt. Accordingly, fct-FeCuPt/Pt NPs show the highest catalytic activity for ORR than those of other five NPs and the commercial Pt catalyst. Thus, the structure-induced surface strain for ORR and many other chemical reactions could be regulated and optimized for catalytic activity.

The significantly enhanced catalytic activity of the fct-FePt/Pt NPs for oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) is attributed to that fct-FePt relieves the over-compressed Pt strain in fcc-FePt according to the quantum mechanics-molecular mechanics (QM-MM) simulations. Furthermore, the Pt strain over catalytic activity of ORR can be enhanced by partially replacing Fe with Cu in FePt. Accordingly, fct-FeCuPt/Pt NPs show the highest catalytic activity for ORR than those of other five NPs and the commercial Pt catalyst. Thus, the structure-induced surface strain for ORR and many other chemical reactions could be regulated and optimized for catalytic activity.
